subject: LU-1330 obdclass: split client-server mount routines

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message:
Patch Set 1:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/5708/

Patch Set 1: Verified

Build Failed 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/5708/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1: Fails

Tests received by maloo, run on el6/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/abdf69bc-993b-11e1-a411-52540035b04c).  Ran 2 tests.  1 tests failed: lustre-initialization-1.

Patch Set 1:

Hi,

I'm trying to work out why this fails to initialize each time.

Chris

Patch Set 1:

The problem is this code in test-framework.sh;

export MKFS=${MKFS:-"$LUSTRE/utils/mkfs.lustre"}
[ ! -f "$MKFS" ] && export MKFS=$(which mkfs.lustre)

Because you don't install mkfs.lustre on the client MKFS ends up undefined. 

The code is flawed because it defines MKFS to be the location of mkfs.lustre on the client even though mkfs.lustre will be run on the server.

You need to resolve this flawed test-framework.sh code

Patch Set 1:

Chris,

Thank you very much for looking into it.

I think the "export MKFS" issue in test-framework.sh has already been handled in my patch: 
238	    if ! client_only; then
239	        export MKFS=${MKFS:-"$LUSTRE/utils/mkfs.lustre"}
240	        [ ! -f "$MKFS" ] && export MKFS=$(which mkfs.lustre)
241	        export TUNEFS=${TUNEFS:-"$LUSTRE/utils/tunefs.lustre"}
242	        [ ! -f "$TUNEFS" ] && export TUNEFS=$(which tunefs.lustre)
243	    fi
The above is executed after checking "export CLIENTMODSONLY=yes", so I think it seems to be not the problem?

I ever meet the issue in my self testing before and the test passed after I changed the test-framework.sh.

From the maloo log, it was failed at server-side mds formating phase.

Thanks!

Patch Set 1:

I am looking at your code and MKFS does not get set to a valid value.

Only 2 lines set MKFS in your code

export MKFS=${MKFS:-"$LUSTRE/utils/mkfs.lustre"}
[ ! -f "$MKFS" ] && export MKFS=$(which mkfs.lustre)

And the result is MKFS="" when MKFS needs to be the path to mkfs.lustre

Patch Set 1:

Sorry I still not understand, MKFS is only needed for server for formating right?(formatall of test-framwork.sh). 
The code "export MKFS=${MKFS:-"$LUSTRE/utils/mkfs.lustre"} [ ! -f "$MKFS" ] && export MKFS=$(which mkfs.lustre)" will not be executed on client-side and can get right value at server-side.

There is a "[ "$CLIENTONLY" ] && return" in "formatall", seems it should be "[ clientonly ] && return"?

Patch Set 1:

OK, it is clear now. MKFS needed to be detected at the testing node (client), the path(real mkfs.lustre) will be remotly executed at server. But server get empty MKFS path and failed the format.

I will update the test-framework.sh for it. May after my previous dependenct patch be merged.

Thanks Chris.
Uploaded patch set 2.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(8 inline comments)

Patch Set 11:

Hi Oleg,

Any chance to get this patch (as well as others of LU-1330) be merged?

LU-1330's purpose is to split some server-side handling to get a relative clean client. The patches is easily to get conflictions and I'm afraid frequently re-submit will waste maloo's bandwidth.

Thanks.

Patch Set 14:

Just a rebase.

LU-1330's patches split large pieces of code to different files. I think it is good for client kernelization because it can reduce future's possible maintain effort between whamcloud tree and linux kernel tree. When we only change server-side code in whamcloud tree, we need not SYNC it to kernel.

Now other patches of LU-1330 are not rebased.

parents.subject: LU-1897 test: replay-single test_70b dbench not found

commit.subject: LU-1330 obdclass: split client-server mount routines
commit.message:
LU-1330 obdclass: split client-server mount routines

Move server side mount routines to obd_mount_server.c.  Const correct
several server_name2xxx type functions.

Signed-off-by: Liu Xuezhao <xuezhao.liu@emc.com>
Signed-off-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Change-Id: I8abdb6fdd0411f2e75f6fb6ee4ff8502e50ef213
